message:
test(cleanup-safety,bd-ofk1n): 3 adversarial symlink cases extending 0a89a96a

Bead coding_agent_session_search-ofk1n (extends 0a89a96a's symlink-
escape hardening with adversarial shapes those two tests do not
exercise directly). Test-only change in the existing
`#[cfg(all(test, unix))] mod cleanup_target_safety_tests`.

The original two tests (0a89a96a) both put a symlink on an ANCESTOR
between data_dir and the candidate — they exercise the
ancestor-walk arm of `cleanup_path_has_symlink_below_root`. The
three new cases cover shapes those tests don't:

1. `cleanup_target_safety_rejects_candidate_that_is_itself_a_symlink`
   The path IS a symlink with all ancestors being regular
   directories. Exercises the symlink_metadata(path)==is_symlink
   arm at the TOP of cleanup_path_has_symlink_below_root — a
   regression that accidentally walked only parent() chain instead
   of starting at `current = path` would slip a direct-symlink
   candidate through.

2. `cleanup_target_safety_rejects_hardlink_alias_pointing_at_db_path`
   An attacker who can create a hardlink alias under data_dir to
   the canonical DB would bypass the string-level db_path check.
   Uses std::fs::hard_link (same-FS guaranteed under tempdir) to
   build a realistic alias; the test accepts either canonicalize-
   rejected (canonical_alias == canonical_db) or the
   index-prefix-rejected branch because the alias lives under
   index_path. Either outcome pins the defense.

3. `cleanup_target_safety_accepts_deep_nested_non_symlinked_candidate`
   Happy-path guard. Without this, future rejection-focused
   hardening could silently become over-rejecting and the existing
   tests (all negative) would still green while
   doctor --fix in production never reclaims anything.

Drive-by discovery (captured in inline comment on test #3):
retained publish backups live ONE level under
`<index_parent>/.lexical-publish-backups` — the directory IS the
retention candidate, not a file under it. Initial draft of this
test had the candidate two levels deep and failed fast, which is
the exact regression signal a future refactor would see if it
moved the layout or the is_retained_publish_backup check.

Verified locally:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_cass_cc_2 \
  rch exec -- cargo test --lib cleanup_target_safety
  -> 5 passed in 0.00s (2 existing + 3 new)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
